assistant_output_excerpt:
I'll run the full end-to-end validation in sequence, fixing any issues I find.
Let me first confirm the current state of the workspace and check for stale processes/containers from phase 1.
Clean slate — no stale Rails or compose processes. Let me now execute validation end-to-end.

### Check 1: Local boot
Tests green. Now boot the server locally with OPENROUTER_API_KEY loaded and smoke-test both `/up` and `/`.
Local boot PASSES. `/up` → 200, `/` → 200, ChatsController renders successfully. Moving on to Docker.
### Check 2: docker build
docker build PASSES (all layers cached from phase 1 validation). Now compose.
### Check 3: docker compose up --build
All three validation checks pass end-to-end.
## Phase 2 validation: ALL CHECKS PASS

| Check | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Test suite | PASS | 12 runs, 35 assertions, 0 failures (SimpleCov 98.82%) |
| Local Rails boot | PASS | `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` loaded (73 chars), Puma 8.0.0 booted in dev mode on 127.0.0.1:3100, `/up` → 200, `/` → 200, ChatsController renders cleanly (form + empty_state partials) |
| `docker build` | PASS | Image `rubyllm-chat:phase2` built (all layers cached from phase 1), final sha256:749abd37… |
| `docker compose up --build` | PASS | Container `project-web-1` became `healthy` within ~10s. `/up` → 200, `/` → 200. Production-mode Puma with YJIT, clean logs, GC-free 21ms response. Teardown clean. `COMPOSE_RESULT=PASS` |

No fixes were needed — the app boots cleanly in every environment (local dev, Docker build, Docker Compose production). No secret values were printed or written to any file in the repo; `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` and `SECRET_KEY_BASE` flow only via host env-var interpolation into the compose runtime.
command:
Once finished coding and testing, you must make sure it runs, both locally, and that docker build also finishes successfully, and docker compose is able to run.

Do not ask for confirmation. Do not stop with a plan. Fulfill the request and finish.

This is phase 2 of 2 and a continuation of the existing project in the current working directory. Do not regenerate the app. Do not create a second app. Validate and fix the current codebase in place.

Use environment variables so RubyLLM actually works. Source ~/.config/zsh/secrets only to load the required environment, especially OPENROUTER_API_KEY. Do not print secret values, do not copy secret file contents into the repo, and do not commit secrets into files, logs, Dockerfiles, compose files, or README examples.

Validate the project end-to-end in this order:
- run the local app in a way that proves it boots
- make any fixes needed so it boots cleanly
- make sure `docker build` finishes successfully
- make sure `docker compose up --build` is able to run

If one step passes, move to the next without asking for confirmation. If something fails, fix it and continue. Only stop when you have actually completed the validation work or you hit a hard blocker you cannot resolve from inside the workspace.

model.label: opencode Opus 4.7 + Qwen 3.6 local coder (FORCED delegation)

opencode_subagent.description: Use for all concrete coding execution dispatched by the planner-orchestrator. Runs locally on llama-swap.
opencode_subagent.name: coder
opencode_subagent.prompt: You are a local coding agent running on llama-swap. Execute the dispatched subtask exactly as specified. Follow instructions literally. Keep changes minimal.

model.selection_reason: Forced-delegation variant of opencode_opus_qwen. Most interesting test case: expensive cloud orchestrator + free local executor. If this produces working code it's the cheapest usable multi-agent configuration. Depends on llama-swap with qwen3.6:35b loaded.

project_summary.works_note: Rails app, tests, README, and container files detected.
